name: Juwayn ibn Malik
title: Lion of Insight
shortDescription: A warrior from the Taym tribe who came to Karbala with Ibn Sa'd's army but, seeing the Imam's proposals rejected, joined him.
fullStory:
Juwayn ibn Malik al-Tamimi was a brave warrior and fighter from the 'Taym al-Lat' tribe. Initially, due to the pressure and threats of Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad, he was forced to leave Kufa with Umar Sa'd's army and come to Karbala. However, his heart was never aligned with falsehood.

In Karbala, when Imam Hussain (AS) offered logical and fair proposals to prevent war, and Umar Sa'd, under Ibn Ziyad's command, rejected all of them, Juwayn realized the deep animosity and malice of the Kufan army. He, along with some of his tribesmen, defected from Umar Sa'd's army at night.

Juwayn took refuge in Imam Hussain's (AS) camp, crossing from the darkness of falsehood to the light of truth. Imam Hussain (AS) welcomed this repentant warrior with open arms.

On the morning of Ashura, Juwayn, whose soul was now washed from the dust of sin, drew his sword with unparalleled bravery. In the first attack of Umar Sa'd's army, he fought manfully shoulder to shoulder with the Imam's other companions.

Finally, this lion of insight, after a fierce battle and killing a number of Kufan soldiers, was targeted by the enemy's vindictive arrows and swords, permanently registering his name in the list of the saved ones of Ashura.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى جُوَيْنِ بْنِ مالِكٍ التَّميمِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Juwayn ibn Malik al-Tamimi.
